audit_items.proof_trace: All heap allocations in rdb.c (zfree, sdsfree, decrRefCount) are correctly paired: in rdbLoadLzfStringObject, 'c' is freed exactly once (on success or error) and 'val' is either returned or freed; rioInitWithFdset memcpy-copies the fds array so zfree(fds) after it is safe; stream loading frees cgname/cname/nodekey after rax/streamCreate functions copy them internally. The double-fclose in rdbSave (L1252->werr->L1277) is a FILE* handle issue, not a heap pointer use-after-free, and is benign on Linux (yields EBADF at worst). Error paths that skip object cleanup lead to exit(1) via rdbExitReportCorruptRDB.
